title: Two-entry infinity norm is nonnegative

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For any two real entries, the two-entry infinity norm max(|x|, |y|) is nonnegative.
informal.latex: For all x,y in R, 0 <= ||(x,y)||_infty := max(|x|,|y|).
assumptions: x and y are real numbers
the infinity norm is defined as max of the two absolute values
informal.expected_conclusion: The two-entry infinity norm is nonnegative.

declarations.name: vec2InfNorm
declarations.statement: def vec2InfNorm (x y : Real) : Real := max |x| |y|

declarations.name: vec2InfNorm_nonneg
declarations.statement: theorem vec2InfNorm_nonneg (x y : Real) : 0 <= vec2InfNorm x y

tasks.input: Translate the nonnegativity of the two-entry infinity norm into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem vec2InfNorm_nonneg (x y : Real) : 0 <= vec2InfNorm x y

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof using nonnegativity of absolute value and the left max bound.
tasks.target: Unfold vec2InfNorm and use le_max_of_le_left with abs_nonneg.
